compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: ALIGNMENT WITH EXISTING EVAL INFRA (do NOT build from scratch). rebar already has: (1) the Inspect-AI/promptfoo EVAL SEAM (workflow WS-G, commit 036f500); (2) a per-criterion plan-review eval suite in src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/*.eval.yaml (built under epic 5fd2 messy-moose-jig, CLOSED) with discrimination/recall fixtures for A1/E2/E5/E6/F4/G5/G6/T2/T12 — but NOT G3/G4 (the container criteria are a COVERAGE GAP); (3) src/rebar/llm/parity.py 'parallel-run-and-diff' (ItemRecord/parity_report/_recall_false_accept: recall+false-accept on a gold set with min_gold certification) — exactly the old-vs-new diff S6 needs. ALIGNS WITH epic 6f2d soupy-spool-revel (OPEN) WS-EVAL-EXISTING — 'parallel-run-and-diff old vs new made into a standing eval' over the DSO/snap-oakhart/rebar corpuses; S6 is a specific instance of that discipline for the container path. ALSO 9da1 sheer-myth-idol (three-pass realign + Inspect seam) and 3156 oaken-golem-rash (project criteria eval-gated). RE-SCOPE S6: extend the eval_specs suite with G3/G4 merged-vs-separate + packed-vs-per-child cases, reuse parity.py's recall metric + the Inspect seam + the shared corpuses; coordinate with 6f2d WS-EVAL-EXISTING rather than duplicate.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: META EVIDENCE from the single-pass G3/G4 review of THIS epic (run as the merge+pack extreme — one subagent over all 6 children instead of the 2N fan-out): evaluating all children TOGETHER IMPROVED cross-child (G4) recall — it surfaced an inter-child gap (S2's unverified prompt relocation) that a one-child-at-a-time fan-out would have MISSED (each child reads fine alone; the gap lives between S2 and S6). This validates the design hypothesis that G3/G4 (cross-child criteria) benefit from seeing children together. CAVEAT: attention skewed to the first/most-annotated children; TAIL children got less granular review (recency/salience bias). => batching preserves/improves cross-child fidelity but risks per-child DEPTH on tail children; the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must also apply to S4. S6's eval should specifically check TAIL-child recall.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (criterion T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety) — findings:

No findings. T11 is an overlay that applies only when a plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills data; this plan does neither.

Affirmations (T11 not-applicable, correctly):
- The optimizations are runtime/in-memory and provider-side: prompt-level caching is Anthropic ephemeral cache (anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions), parallelizing/merging/bin-packing the fan-out are runtime concerns, and S2's "relocating per-run ticket data out of the system prompt" is a prompt-assembly refactor — none introduce or alter a persisted format.
- The one on-disk artifact (the per-ticket checkpoint cache) is explicitly disposable/rebuildable and material-fingerprint-keyed (a changed fingerprint cold-misses and ignores the stale entry — sizing.load_checkpoint), so there is no backfill, no blocking DDL, and no data-loss surface to guard. No expand-contract, batching, resumability, dual-write, or rollback obligations attach.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T3 Integration feasibility) — PASS, no findings.

The external capability the plan is load-bearing on (pydantic-ai anthropic prompt caching) is real and already wired: AnthropicModelSettings(anthropic_cache_instructions=True, anthropic_cache_tool_definitions=True) at src/rebar/llm/runner.py:421-435, anthropic-gated via _anthropic_cache_settings(), and unit-tested (tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:315,390). The RunnerAgentStep shared seam (workflow/runs.py:94), eval_specs (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/), and the parity harness the plan reuses all exist. Provider-safety (openai/gemini unchanged) is concretely satisfied by the resolved-model gate. No SPIKE warranted.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification). Findings + affirmations below.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 criteria this chunk passes):
- (a) Relevant prior art IS considered before committing. The caching approach is explicitly grounded in OSS pydantic-ai users ("Caching approach + gotchas confirmed against OSS pydantic-ai users (Arize/phoenix, code-graph-rag, shotgun, varro)"), and the implementation reuses an EXISTING seam — `src/rebar/llm/runner.py:421 _anthropic_cache_settings` + `runner.py:210-220` already implement anthropic-gated `anthropic_cache_instructions`/`anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` with the no-RunRequest-change discipline the plan's S1 remediation calls for. The plan is repackaging/extending a real, tested capability, not reinventing it.
- (a) S6's prior-art distinction is sound and verifiable: it correctly separates the EXISTING structural parity harness (FakeRunner, zero model calls) from a NEW semantic eval, and reuses the established Inspect-AI eval seam (`src/rebar/llm/eval.py`, `eval_specs/`) rather than inventing a parallel one.
- (a) The Out-of-scope section explicitly rejects two alternative designs (pre-ca03 "2 parallel whole-plan calls"; content chunking) with rationale — evidence the design space and prior art were weighed.
- (b) Provenance (story ca03 / commit 832b3af0) is cited for the existing per-child fan-out, so the change is positioned against a known prior design rather than a greenfield invention.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Justify the child bin-packing approach (S5) against established prior art or state why a bespoke packer is warranted (Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging).
  criteria: [T1]
  location: "Children" list item 5 (S5 child bin-packing) + Acceptance Criteria
  evidence: ["The plan names S5 'child bin-packing' and gates it on S4/S6, but unlike the caching work (which cites Arize/phoenix, code-graph-rag, shotgun, varro) it offers NO prior-art consideration for the bin-packing algorithm itself — bin-packing is a well-studied problem (first-fit-decreasing etc.) and the plan should state whether it adopts a standard heuristic or a bespoke one, and why.", "sizing.py is the named home for container-cost projections (Sequencing note: 'S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections'), so the packing policy is load-bearing on cost correctness."]
  suggested_fix: "In the S5 description, name the packing heuristic (e.g. first-fit-decreasing by projected token size) and note it is a standard bin-packing approach adapted to the sizing.py cost model, OR justify a bespoke packer."

- [ ] Resolve the unverified capability assertion that warm-then-fan-out reuses a warmed prompt cache across the new in-`_run_container` pool (S3) — confirm anthropic prompt-cache TTL/scope covers the fan-out window, not just that the setting is enabled.
  criteria: [T1]
  location: "Remediation (opus review)" — S3 bullet ("non-streaming warm-then-fan-out (warm = first real pairing to completion)")
  evidence: ["S3's whole token-savings premise is that the first 'warm' pairing populates a cache that subsequent parallel pairings READ. runner.py:210-220 enables the cache breakpoint, but the plan asserts (not verifies) that the warmed system-prompt cache is HIT by later sibling calls within the fan-out — anthropic prompt caching has a 5-minute default TTL and is keyed on exact prefix bytes. This is a load-bearing capability claim ('cache reads non-zero' is an AC) that the plan text does not resolve.", "AC: 'materially lower token cost (cache reads non-zero)'"]
  suggested_fix: ""

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review note (G6): The S1 caching substrate (anthropic system-prompt + tool-definition caching applied at the shared runner seam, gated to anthropic providers, no RunRequest content-list change) appears to ALREADY EXIST in the live code as story 0250 — see runner.py:209-222 and _anthropic_cache_settings() at runner.py:421-435 (anthropic_cache_instructions / anthropic_cache_tool_definitions). The plan's S1 ("caching substrate at the shared runner seam (fidelity-neutral) — foundational") and the Remediation "S1 RESCOPED: cache the SYSTEM PROMPT (anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions, anthropic-gated)... no RunRequest content-list change" read as describing exactly what 0250 already shipped. Please clarify in the plan what S1 ADDS beyond 0250 (e.g. is it now a no-op verification, or a distinct piece such as cross-call cache-window TTL / breakpoint placement / metrics)? As written, S2/S3 are gated on S1 which may already be done — risking a redundant or under-specified foundational story.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness). Affirmations: S1 cache mechanism verified correct & fit-for-purpose against code (parent plan lives in system_prompt at passes.py:257; anthropic-gated, provider-safe runner seam at runner.py:421-435; no RunRequest content-list change preserves the structured-output retry path) — positive rationale stated throughout. S3's 'don't shed G3/G4 + fix cost model' is grounded (shed_to_budget sheds container at sizing.py:221-226; project() multiplies container by n_children at line 206) and the strict S3->S4->S5 sequencing note is well-reasoned. Approach selection (thread-pool + warm-then-fan-out, eval-gated batching) is defensible. One coaching finding: the plan adds 'a NEW pool inside _run_container' but does not specify how the parallel fan-out aggregates results safely — _run_container currently mutates a shared `out` via out.extend() and reads len(out) for per-pairing counts (pass1.py:104,106,125,142); the concurrent aggregation path should be specified (futures->ordered collect, not shared-list mutation).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: FORCE_CLAIM: plan-review gate bypassed by user approval — no plan-review attestation was verified. Reason: "Eval-harness story; implementing with subagent code review as binding quality gate; gate review is the slow path this epic optimizes.".

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion G6 (approach soundness, anti-patterns & alternative-selection): CLEAN, no findings.

Affirmations (criteria this plan satisfies for G6):
- MECHANISM CORRECTNESS: verified against code. The plan's load-bearing claim that the stable parent plan lives in the SYSTEM PROMPT (not the user message) is correct — `passes.pass1_container` builds the RunRequest with `system_prompt=_resolve_system(PASS_CONTAINER, parent_plan, cfg)` (passes.py:257) while the per-child whole content is in `instructions` (passes.py:258-264); caching is applied anthropic-gated at the shared runner seam on that system block (runner.py:209-222). So S1's "cache the system prompt, anthropic_cache_instructions" is mechanically sound and avoids touching the structured-output retry path.
- The warm-then-fan-out is the correct fix for the real hazard: a naive concurrent fan-out would make every pairing MISS+WRITE the whole-parent cache prefix (~20x input, no read benefit). Warming gate (`parent_tokens >= CACHE_MIN_PREFIX_TOKENS and len(pairings) >= 2`, pass1.py:165) plus a fan-out READ pool is logic-complete. Edge/failure cases are handled explicitly: too-big pairing -> DET failure finding with no LLM call (pass1.py:149-150); systemic LLMUnavailableError on the WARM call aborts the tier (pass1.py:171-182); non-systemic warm failure degrades to direct all-pairings fan-out and the failed pairing re-runs in the pool (no silent drop, pass1.py:183-205); per-pairing failure in the pool drops only that pairing's findings, matching the sequential baseline (pass1.py:222-241). The aggregate finding set equals the sequential baseline (no dup/drop) — no hidden ordering/atomicity assumption.
- FITNESS-FOR-PURPOSE: attacks the named problem (sequential 2*N_children wall-time + re-sent parent plan), not a proxy — parallelization addresses wall-clock, system-prompt caching addresses re-sent token cost, and S6's semantic eval harness directly guards the fidelity AC.
- BUDGET-CAP fix is sound and fidelity-preserving: G3/G4 are NEVER shed (sizing.py:198-206, 232-242); the cap bounds only sheddable single-turn/agent/overlay spend, with the container fan-out modeled as a fixed cost floor. This is the cost-model fix, not centrality-inversion.
- APPROACH SELECTION: reviewer-generated structural alternatives are all addressed/dominated. (a) "2 parallel whole-plan calls" — explicitly out-of-scope with a stated reason (trades back per-child fidelity). (b) cache the user message — the plan correctly rejects this (would break the structured-output retry path) and places the stable bytes in the system prompt instead. (c) invert centrality scaling to keep spend under cap — explicitly rejected because it would shed G3/G4 first. Chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on codebase-alignment, blast-radius (one opaque `batch` step; interpreter/YAML untouched), testability (eval-gated S4/S5 + parity harness), simplicity, and robustness.
- POSITIVE RATIONALE present throughout: the Context, Remediation, and Sequencing-note sections state WHY the shared-runner seam, WHY system-prompt caching, WHY warm-then-fan-out, WHY the cost-model fix, and WHY the de-risk DAG ordering (fidelity-neutral pieces ship ahead of eval-gated S4/S5).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (criterion T11 — Data-migration / backfill safety) — no findings.

T11 is an OVERLAY that applies only when a plan changes a schema / persisted format or backfills data; this plan does neither. Verified against the code, not just the plan text.

Affirmations (T11 correctly not-applicable):
- All optimizations are runtime/in-memory or provider-side: S1 = Anthropic ephemeral prompt/system-prompt caching (anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions, runner.py:421-435); S3 = parallelize/warm-then-fan-out the container fan-out (concurrency, no persisted state); S2 = "relocating per-run ticket data out of the system prompt" is a prompt-ASSEMBLY refactor (no on-disk format); S4/S5 = merge G3+G4 / bin-pack children (call structuring); S6 = add eval_specs fixtures (test data, not a runtime persisted format). None introduce or alter a persisted schema.
- The only on-disk artifact is the per-ticket checkpoint cache (.rebar/cache/plan-review/<ticket_id>/), and it carries NO migration obligation: it is git-ignored and disposable (_checkpoint_dir, sizing.py:246-251); content-keyed on the ticket MATERIAL fingerprint + criterion ids + model + tier so any material change cold-misses and ignores the stale entry (_checkpoint_key, sizing.py:254-259); written atomically (tmp + rename, save_checkpoint sizing.py:287-299); and best-effort on both read and write (any error ⇒ recompute / proceed, load_checkpoint/save_checkpoint). There is no backfill, no blocking DDL on a large table, no dual-write window, and no data-loss surface to guard.

No expand-contract, batching/throttling, resumability, dual-write, or rollback obligations attach. T11 PASSES as not-applicable.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T3 (Integration feasibility) — CLEAN (no findings).

The plan integrates pydantic-ai's anthropic prompt-caching capability (an external-library capability the plan depends on), so T3 applies. Verified MATCH against the codebase rather than asserted-but-unverified:

AFFIRM [T3]:
- (a) technical_feasibility — achievable as described; no capability gap. The load-bearing primitives already exist and are exercised: `_anthropic_cache_settings` emits `anthropic_cache_instructions`/`anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` on `AnthropicModelSettings` (src/rebar/llm/runner.py:421-435), warm-then-fan-out container is implemented (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py:114-200), `RunnerAgentStep` is the real shared seam (src/rebar/llm/workflow/runs.py:94).
- (b) named settings keys exist — MATCH. `anthropic_cache_instructions` / `anthropic_cache_tool_definitions` are real `AnthropicModelSettings` fields (runner.py:430-435; tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:315-316,390-391); `cache_read_tokens`/`cache_write_tokens` are captured off pydantic-ai `RunUsage` (runner.py:399-417; test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:324-339), so the "cache reads non-zero" AC is measurable.
- (c) auth/HTTPS/provider preconditions stated — caching is gated to anthropic-resolved model strings and is a no-op (no cache_* sent) on openai/gemini (runner.py:421-429), directly satisfying the "provider-safe: openai/gemini calls succeed unchanged" AC; non-anthropic local-server config fails loudly via `_pai_check_config` (runner.py:438-450).
- (d) no critical capability gap → no SPIKE required. The S2 precondition is correctly identified and code-grounded: the workflow system prompt interpolates per-run ticket data (runs.py:131-146), which is exactly why the plan scopes S2 to "relocating per-run ticket data out of the (interpolated) system prompt."

ANTI-FP note: did not flag any mismatch — every named external capability verifies against existing code/tests.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review (T1 — prior-art / novel-architecture justification). Independent review; corroborates the earlier T1/G6 notes and adds one grounded finding. Bright-lines that fire: performance/scalability target + a novel-sounding pattern ("warm-then-fan-out") + a migration of bespoke-harness code.

AFFIRMATIONS (T1 criteria this chunk passes):
- (a) Relevant prior art IS considered for the caching design: the plan grounds it in OSS pydantic-ai users ("confirmed against OSS pydantic-ai users (Arize/phoenix, code-graph-rag, shotgun, varro)") and reuses an EXISTING seam rather than inventing one.
- (a) S6 cleanly separates prior art from the gap: it reuses the EXISTING structural parity harness (parity.py, FakeRunner, zero model calls) + the established Inspect-AI eval seam + eval_specs/, and correctly identifies that a NEW *semantic* eval is required (G3/G4 are an eval-coverage gap) — repackaging-aware.
- (a) Out-of-scope explicitly weighs and rejects two alternative designs (pre-ca03 "2 parallel whole-plan calls"; content chunking) with rationale.
- (b) Provenance is cited for the prior design (story ca03 / commit 832b3af0) so the change is positioned against a known prior, not greenfield.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Reconcile S1 (and the warm-then-fan-out half of S3) against the code that ALREADY SHIPS this exact mechanism — name stories 0250 and ba7e as prior art and state what each child ADDS, so S1 is not a reinvention/redundant foundational story that gates S2/S3.
  criteria: [T1]
  location: "Children" item 1 (S1) + "Remediation (opus review)" S1/S3 bullets
  evidence: ["S1 ('caching substrate at the shared runner seam... foundational') and the remediation ('S1 RESCOPED: cache the SYSTEM PROMPT (anthropic_cache_instructions/tool_definitions, anthropic-gated)... no RunRequest content-list change') describe code that already exists: src/rebar/llm/runner.py:209-222 + _anthropic_cache_settings() at runner.py:421-435 implement anthropic-gated anthropic_cache_instructions/anthropic_cache_tool_definitions with the no-RunRequest-change discipline, attributed to story 0250 (also unit-tested: tests/unit/test_pydantic_ai_runner.py:307,315,390).", "The S3 'warm-then-fan-out' pattern + 'a NEW pool inside _run_container' also already exist: pass1.py:114-167 documents WARM-THEN-FAN-OUT as 'story ba7e', with CACHE_MIN_PREFIX_TOKENS (pass1.py:47, 'story ba7e') and _CONTAINER_MAX_WORKERS (pass1.py:50). T1(a): the plan risks repackaging shipped work as new children without saying what is left to build."]
  suggested_fix: "Add a one-line prior-art note to S1/S3: 'the anthropic system-prompt/tool-def cache (story 0250, runner.py:421-435) and the warm-then-fan-out pool (story ba7e, pass1.py:114-167) already exist; S1 = <verify/metrics/TTL window?>, S3 = <budget-cap cost-model fix + container-never-shed>'. If S1's net-new work is only verification, mark it so S2/S3 are not gated on a no-op."

- [ ] Resolve the load-bearing capability assertion that the warmed prompt cache is HIT by later parallel pairings within the fan-out window (S3) — confirm anthropic prompt-cache TTL/exact-prefix scope covers the fan-out, not merely that the setting is enabled.
  criteria: [T1]
  location: "Remediation (opus review)" S3 bullet ("non-streaming warm-then-fan-out (warm = first real pairing to completion)")
  evidence: ["S3's entire token-savings premise is that the first 'warm' pairing populates a cache that subsequent parallel pairings READ; runner.py:210-220 enables the breakpoint but the plan asserts (does not verify) the warmed system-prompt prefix is re-HIT by later sibling calls. anthropic prompt caching has a ~5-minute default TTL and is keyed on exact prefix bytes — and 'cache reads non-zero' is an explicit AC.", "AC: 'materially lower token cost (cache reads non-zero)'"]
  suggested_fix: ""

- [ ] Justify S5 child bin-packing against established prior art (bin-packing is well-studied) — name the heuristic adopted (e.g. first-fit-decreasing by projected token size) or state why a bespoke packer is warranted (Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging).
  criteria: [T1]
  location: "Children" item 5 (S5 child bin-packing) + Sequencing note
  evidence: ["The plan names S5 'child bin-packing' but, unlike the caching work, offers NO prior-art consideration for the packing algorithm itself; bin-packing has standard heuristics. The Sequencing note makes the packing policy load-bearing on cost correctness ('S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections')."]
  suggested_fix: "In S5, name the packing heuristic (e.g. first-fit-decreasing over projected per-pairing token size against the sizing.py largest-window budget) and note it is a standard approach adapted to the cost model, OR justify a bespoke packer."

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe). This plan defines an LLM/agent review system (G3/G4 container reviewers, a structured `plan_review_findings` output schema, and S4's "merge G3+G4 into one call" + S5's "bin-pack children" prompt restructurings), so T8 applies. Findings + affirmations below; tool-verified against the live container/finder code.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 checklist items this plan satisfies):
- Referenced agents/seams/enums EXIST and are real (no pink-elephant): `_run_container` (pass1.py:114), `pass1_container` (passes.py:241), the shared `RunRequest`/runner seam + `_anthropic_cache_settings` (runner.py:209-222,421-435), `output_schema="plan_review_findings"` (passes.py:209,268), the eval_specs suite (eval_specs/*.eval.yaml) and `parity.py` are all present and named.
- FALLBACK for a failed sub-step IS specified for the EXISTING path: warm-pairing systemic failure aborts the tier; non-systemic warm failure degrades to direct fan-out with the failed pairing re-run in the pool (no silent drop); per-pairing pool failure drops only that pairing's findings (pass1.py:171-241). S6 also specifies an eval fallback posture (offline discipline blocks; live eval opt-in/non-blocking).
- No ambiguous counter/state increment in the plan-level spec: the cost-model/budget-cap fix is described as "never shed G3/G4" + "fix the cost model" (not centrality scaling), which is unambiguous at plan grain.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] S4 ("merge G3+G4 into one call per bin") changes the per-call criterion vocabulary from ONE criterion to TWO, but the plan never specifies the FINDING→CRITERION ATTRIBUTION PROTOCOL for the merged call (how the merged prompt instructs the model to tag each finding G3-vs-G4 in the structured `criteria` field, and the keep/drop rule). State this protocol in S4.
  criteria: [T8]
  location: "Children" item 4 (S4) + Remediation "merge G3+G4 into one call per bin"; AC "Findings remain within offline-eval tolerance"; ticket comment "G3/G4-attribution accuracy" (S6 metric)
  evidence: ["The existing single-criterion path (`pass1_container`, passes.py:241-270) passes ONE `criterion` and so cannot mis-attribute. The MULTI-criterion finder path proves attribution is load-bearing: passes.py:214-224 keeps ONLY findings whose model-returned `criteria` intersect the in-chunk ids and DROPS the rest — with an explicit warning that the old `or ids[:1]` fallback 'silently mis-attributed it to the chunk's first criterion, corrupting the finding→criterion mapping the coach + sidecar depend on' (passes.py:217-220).", "S6's own AC requires reporting 'G3/G4-attribution accuracy' — i.e. attribution is a measured success dimension — yet the plan text gives no protocol the executor must implement to produce a correctly-attributed merged finding set.", "ABSENCE: no S4 description text in the plan specifies the merged-call prompt's per-criterion tagging instruction or the post-call keep/drop/attribution rule."]
  suggested_fix: "In S4, specify that the merged G3+G4 prompt requires each finding to carry the exact criterion id (G3 or G4) in `criteria`, and that the post-call handler mirrors the finder's discipline (passes.py:222): keep only findings whose `criteria` ⊆ {G3,G4}, never fabricate an attribution to a default criterion."

- [ ] The "REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail" that S6's review notes is ESSENTIAL and "must also apply to S4" is referenced but its SCHEMA/PROTOCOL is never co-located with (or defined in) the plan — specify the guardrail's shape (the per-child structured output it enforces) where S4/S5 use it.
  criteria: [T8]
  location: "Children" items 4-5 (S4 merge / S5 bin-pack); ticket comment (1782572466917749001) "the REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail (already in S5) is ESSENTIAL and must also apply to S4"
  evidence: ["When children are batched (S4 merge / S5 bin-pack), multiple children share ONE call; the meta-review of this very epic observed TAIL children get shallower review (recency/salience bias) and concluded a structured per-child output guardrail is required to force per-child coverage. The plan text names no such guardrail and does not define its schema (e.g. a required finding-or-explicit-clean entry per child id) — a processing rule referenced but not co-located with the schema that needs it.", "ABSENCE: the plan's S5 line is only 'child bin-packing' and S4 is only 'merge G3+G4 into one call per bin'; neither states the per-child completeness guarantee or its structured shape."]
  suggested_fix: "Specify in S4/S5 that each batched call must return a structured per-child result keyed by child ticket_id (a finding list OR an explicit 'clean' marker per child), so a batched call cannot silently omit a tail child; have S6's eval check tail-child recall against this guarantee."

- [ ] Specify the FALLBACK when a merged/packed call exceeds the window in S4/S5 — the existing too-big rule fires per (parent+ONE child) pairing (pass1.py:146-150); merging criteria and packing multiple children per call changes the pairing size and the per-criterion/per-child failure granularity, which the plan leaves unspecified.
  criteria: [T8]
  location: "Children" items 4-5 (S4/S5); Remediation; Sequencing note ("S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections")
  evidence: ["Today an oversized pairing becomes a DET 'too-big' failure finding (pass1.py:149-150, `_too_big_finding`) at single-child granularity. A merged G3+G4 / multi-child bin raises the bin's token size and conflates failure attribution: if a packed bin is too big or its call fails, the plan does not say whether it (a) splits the bin, (b) emits a single too-big finding, or (c) attributes the failure across the merged criteria / packed children.", "ABSENCE: no S4/S5 text defines the incomplete-bin fallback or how a per-bin failure maps back to specific (criterion, child) results."]
  suggested_fix: "In S5, define a deterministic bin-split/fallback (e.g. on too-big or call failure, re-run the bin's children as smaller bins / per-child pairings) so no child or criterion is silently dropped; in S4 state how a merged-call failure is recorded against both G3 and G4."

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion T8 (LLM/prompt structural-completeness probe). This plan defines an LLM/agent review system (G3/G4 container reviewers, the structured `plan_review_findings` output schema, and S4 "merge G3+G4 into one call per bin" + S5 "bin-pack children" prompt restructurings), so T8 applies. Tool-verified against the live container/finder code. Independent corroboration of the earlier T8 note (1782578526685470001) — facts re-confirmed below.

AFFIRMATIONS (T8 checklist items this plan satisfies):
- Referenced agents/seams/enums EXIST (no pink-elephant): `_run_container` (pass1.py:114), `pass1_container` (passes.py:241), the shared RunRequest/runner seam + `_anthropic_cache_settings` (runner.py:209-222,421-435), `output_schema="plan_review_findings"` (passes.py:209,268), `CACHE_MIN_PREFIX_TOKENS`/`_CONTAINER_MAX_WORKERS` (pass1.py:47,50), eval_specs/*.eval.yaml + parity.py — all present and named.
- FALLBACK for a failed sub-step IS specified for the EXISTING path: warm-pairing systemic failure aborts the tier; non-systemic warm failure degrades to direct fan-out with the failed pairing re-run in the pool (no silent drop); per-pairing pool failure drops only that pairing's findings (pass1.py:171-241). S6 specifies an eval fallback posture (offline discipline blocks; live eval opt-in/non-blocking).
- No ambiguous counter/state increment at plan grain: the budget-cap fix is "never shed G3/G4" + "fix the cost model" (not centrality scaling) — unambiguous.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Specify the FINDING→CRITERION ATTRIBUTION PROTOCOL for S4's merged call. S4 ("merge G3+G4 into one call per bin") changes the per-call vocabulary from ONE criterion to TWO, but the plan never states how the merged prompt instructs the model to tag each finding G3-vs-G4 in the structured `criteria` field, nor the keep/drop rule.
  criteria: [T8]
  location: "Children" item 4 (S4) + Remediation "merge G3+G4 into one call per bin"; AC "Findings remain within offline-eval tolerance"; S6 metric "G3/G4-attribution accuracy"
  evidence: ["The existing single-criterion path `pass1_container` (passes.py:253 `cid = criterion['id']`, single `## Criterion {cid}`) cannot mis-attribute. The MULTI-criterion finder path proves attribution is load-bearing: passes.py:222-224 keeps ONLY findings whose model-returned `criteria` intersect the in-chunk ids and DROPS the rest, with the comment that the old `or ids[:1]` fallback 'silently mis-attributed it to the chunk's first criterion, corrupting the finding→criterion mapping the coach + sidecar depend on' (passes.py:218-220).", "S6's own AC requires reporting 'G3/G4-attribution accuracy' — attribution is a measured success dimension — yet no plan text gives the protocol the executor must implement.", "ABSENCE: no S4 text specifies the merged-call per-criterion tagging instruction or the post-call keep/drop/attribution rule."]
  suggested_fix: "In S4, require each finding from the merged G3+G4 prompt to carry the exact criterion id (G3 or G4) in `criteria`, and have the post-call handler mirror passes.py:222 — keep only findings whose `criteria` ⊆ {G3,G4}, never default-attribute."

- [ ] Define the SCHEMA of the "REQUIRED structured per-child output guardrail" referenced (comment 1782572466917749001) as ESSENTIAL and "must also apply to S4." It is referenced but its shape/protocol is never co-located with or defined in the plan.
  criteria: [T8]
  location: "Children" items 4-5 (S4 merge / S5 bin-pack); ticket comment 1782572466917749001
  evidence: ["When children are batched (S4/S5), multiple children share ONE call; the meta-review of this epic observed TAIL children get shallower review (recency/salience bias) and concluded a structured per-child output guardrail is required to force per-child coverage. The plan names no such guardrail and does not define its schema — a processing rule referenced but not co-located with the schema that needs it.", "ABSENCE: S5 is only 'child bin-packing' and S4 is only 'merge G3+G4 into one call per bin'; neither states the per-child completeness guarantee or its structured shape."]
  suggested_fix: "Specify that each batched call returns a structured per-child result keyed by child ticket_id (a finding list OR an explicit 'clean' marker per child), so a batched call cannot silently omit a tail child; have S6's eval check tail-child recall against this guarantee."

- [ ] Specify the FALLBACK when a merged/packed call exceeds the window or fails in S4/S5. The existing too-big rule fires per (parent+ONE child) pairing (pass1.py:144-152) and emits a DET failure finding at single-child granularity (`_too_big_finding`, pass1.py:53,149-150); merging criteria and packing multiple children per call changes the bin size and per-criterion/per-child failure granularity, which the plan leaves unspecified.
  criteria: [T8]
  location: "Children" items 4-5 (S4/S5); Remediation; Sequencing note ("S4/S5's sizing.py container-cost projections")
  evidence: ["Today an oversized pairing becomes a DET too-big failure finding at single-child granularity (pass1.py:149-150). A merged G3+G4 / multi-child bin raises bin token size and conflates failure attribution; the plan does not say whether an oversized/failed bin (a) splits, (b) emits one too-big finding, or (c) attributes failure across merged criteria / packed children.", "ABSENCE: no S4/S5 text defines the incomplete-bin fallback or how a per-bin failure maps back to specific (criterion, child) results."]
  suggested_fix: "In S5, define a deterministic bin-split/fallback (on too-big or call failure, re-run the bin's children as smaller bins / per-child pairings) so no child/criterion is silently dropped; in S4 state how a merged-call failure is recorded against both G3 and G4."

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G3 — child coverage), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization), child under review = S6/da34 (fidelity-eval harness). 4-bucket audit of the parent's acceptance/success criteria against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762, S6 da34).

COVERAGE MAP (parent criterion -> bucket / covering child):
- AC1 "all six children closed" -> STRUCTURAL (aggregate; not a single-child obligation).
- AC2 "8-child review in minutes / lower token cost / cache reads non-zero" -> FULLY by siblings S3 (parallelize) + S1 (cache reads) + S4/S5 (call reduction); NOT S6's job — no absence.
- AC3 "caching provider-safe (openai/gemini unchanged)" -> FULLY by siblings S1 + S2 — no absence.
- AC4 / Success "findings remain within OFFLINE-eval tolerance of current per-child behavior / preserve-or-improve fidelity (no regression)" -> S6 is the SOLE/named fidelity instrument => see FINDING below (PARTIAL).
- AC5 "make lint + full suite pass" -> FULLY by S6's own AC6 (and each sibling's lint/test AC).

No FALSE absence: every parent criterion S6 does not own is covered by a named sibling.

FINDING (G3 — partial coverage / qualifier mismatch on the parent fidelity criterion):
- [ ] The parent's fidelity acceptance criterion is worded "Findings remain within OFFLINE-eval tolerance of the current per-child behavior," but S6 — the only child that can cover it — builds the actual fidelity measurement (finding-recall + G3/G4-attribution vs baseline, via parity.py) as the LIVE SEMANTIC eval, which S6 explicitly makes OPT-IN / workflow_dispatch / NON-BLOCKING and "NEVER a required CI status check." S6's OFFLINE check is the deterministic spec/grader-DISCIPLINE check (no model, no finding recall) — it does NOT measure fidelity. So the parent AC4 as literally worded ("within offline-eval tolerance") is covered by NO child: the offline check measures spec validity, not fidelity, and the fidelity measure is live + non-blocking.
  criteria: [G3]
  location: parent c81c "Acceptance Criteria" bullet 4 ("Findings remain within offline-eval tolerance...") + "Success Criteria"; child da34 "CI posture" + ACs 1/2/4; sibling 98c6/1762 "CI/eval posture (S6)" ("HUMAN-REVIEWED development gate... NOT a required/blocking CI check").
  evidence: ["Parent AC4: 'Findings remain within offline-eval tolerance of the current per-child behavior (no fidelity regression).'", "da34 AC1: offline discipline check is 'deterministic, runs on every PR' — spec validity + grader discipline, explicitly 'no model'.", "da34 AC4: 'The LIVE semantic eval is OPT-IN (workflow_dispatch / path-scoped) and NON-BLOCKING — NOT a required CI status check; documented as a human-reviewed development gate for S4/S5.'", "98c6 remediation: 'Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness (DEPENDS ON S6), not a vague offline eval.'"]
  scenarios: ["Epic closes with S6's offline discipline check green on every PR but the live semantic fidelity eval never run (opt-in/non-blocking) -> parent AC4 ('within offline-eval tolerance') is reportable as MET while no finding-fidelity comparison was actually performed; a fidelity regression in S4/S5 ships unmeasured.", "Verifier reads parent AC4 literally, looks for an OFFLINE fidelity-tolerance gate, finds only an offline SPEC-DISCIPLINE check + a live non-blocking eval -> cannot point to any child that closes AC4 as worded."]
  impact: "The parent's headline fidelity criterion (the whole reason S4/S5 are eval-gated) is only PARTIALLY covered by its sole intended consumer: S6 supplies the fidelity measure but reclassifies it as live + non-blocking, while the on-every-PR OFFLINE check it does run measures spec discipline, not findings. The parent AC as literally worded ('offline-eval tolerance') maps to nothing the roster enforces, so 'no fidelity regression' can be marked satisfied without ever measuring fidelity.";
  checklist_item: "The union of children covers each parent acceptance/success criterion — 4-bucket audit; an uncovered criterion is a finding."
  suggested_fix: "Reconcile the parent AC4 wording with S6's actual mechanism: either (a) re-word parent AC4 to 'within SEMANTIC-eval tolerance, measured by the S6 live recall/attribution diff (human-reviewed dev gate before merging S4/S5)' so the criterion names the live, non-blocking instrument that S6 actually builds; and/or (b) add to S6 the explicit obligation that the live semantic eval MUST be run and pass-within-tolerance as the gate for closing S4 and S5 (so 'no fidelity regression' is an enforced gate, not an optional one). Keep S6's offline discipline check as spec-validity only, distinct from the fidelity tolerance."

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
CONTAINER review (G4 — child consistency), parent c81c (plan-review fan-out optimization). Reviewed THIS child (S6/da34, fidelity-eval harness) WHOLE against the complete sibling roster (S1 0250, S2 c6e5, S3 ba7e, S4 98c6, S5 1762) and the parent. Walked all 7 cross-child interaction modes. ONE G4 FINDING (consistency: dependency-strength / conflicting-assumptions mismatch between S6 and its dependents S4/S5).

AFFIRMATIONS (modes that are clean):
- Implicit shared state: S6 owns eval_specs/ + .github/workflows/eval.yml; it REUSES parity.py's recall/false-accept API (also reused by S2 for relocated-prompt fidelity-verify) — shared consumption of the same harness, not a contradictory edit. Benign.
- Scope overlap: S6 explicitly fences out the S4/S5 implementations it gates ("Out: the merge (S4) / bin-pack (S5) implementations it gates"); it only builds specs + diffs the candidate path. Clean producer→consumer split. No overlap.
- Dependency gap / ordering: S6's `blocks` links on S4 (98c6) and S5 (1762) match the parent DAG S6->{S4,S5}; S4/S5 each reciprocally declare "Fidelity gate = the S6 semantic eval harness (DEPENDS ON S6)". Direction is correct; S6 precedes the gated children. (S6 is neither in the parent's "ship ahead" list nor its "eval-gated" list in the one sentence "Fidelity-neutral pieces (S1, S3, S2) ship ahead of the eval-gated ones (S4, S5)" — but the DAG resolves S6's position as the gate, so this is benign, not a finding.)
- Consumer impact: S6's baseline = "current separate-call (G3,G4)/one-child-per-call findings" is precisely the pre-S4/S5 behavior it diffs against; S3's warm-then-fan-out / never-shed changes are fidelity-neutral and don't invalidate that baseline. No stale-consumer contradiction.
- Residual references: S6 correctly distinguishes the EXISTING structural parity harness (FakeRunner, zero model calls) from the NEW semantic eval. No sibling holds a stale contract against this.

FINDING (G4 — conflicting assumptions / dependency strength: S4 & S5 treat S6's eval as a BINDING gate; S6 defines that same eval as OPT-IN / NON-BLOCKING with no run-and-pass obligation):

- finding: S4 (98c6) and S5 (1762) each carry an acceptance criterion that makes S6's live semantic eval a HARD pass-before-close gate ("The S6 fidelity harness shows merged-call/packed-call findings within tolerance of separate/one-child calls"), and their shared "CI/eval posture (S6)" calls it "a HUMAN-REVIEWED development gate run before merge." But S6's OWN acceptance criteria classify that identical live semantic eval as OPT-IN (workflow_dispatch / path-scoped) and NON-BLOCKING — "NOT a required CI status check" — and S6 carries NO acceptance criterion obligating that the live eval actually be RUN and PASS-within-tolerance as the condition for closing S4/S5. S6 guarantees the eval is BUILT and AVAILABLE; the dependents assume it is ENFORCED. The dependency strength S4/S5 rely on (a binding fidelity gate) is stronger than the contract S6 actually offers (an available, non-blocking, opt-in tool). On the current ACs, S4/S5 can close with S6's offline discipline check green while the live fidelity diff is never run — i.e. the eval-gating the parent makes the whole reason S4/S5 are sequenced after S6 is not enforced by any child's AC.
  criteria: [G4]
  location: child da34 ACs 4 (live eval OPT-IN / NON-BLOCKING / "NOT a required CI status check") + the "CI posture" section; sibling 98c6 AC "The S6 fidelity harness shows merged-call findings within tolerance of separate G3/G4 calls" + "CI/eval posture (S6)"; sibling 1762 AC "The S6 fidelity harness shows packed-call findings within tolerance of one-child-per-call" + "CI/eval posture (S6)"; parent c81c "Remediation (opus review)" ("S4 and S5 now DEPEND ON S6") + Sequencing note.
  evidence: ["da34 AC4: 'The LIVE semantic eval is OPT-IN (workflow_dispatch / path-scoped) and NON-BLOCKING — NOT a required CI status check; documented as a human-reviewed development gate for S4/S5.'", "da34 has no AC of the form 'the live semantic eval MUST be run and pass within tolerance to close S4/S5' — it only obligates that the eval EXISTS, reuses parity.py, defines a tolerance, and is non-blocking.", "98c6 AC: 'The S6 fidelity harness shows merged-call findings within tolerance of separate G3/G4 calls.' + 98c6 'CI/eval posture (S6)': 'a HUMAN-REVIEWED development gate run before merge ... NOT a required/blocking CI check.'", "1762 AC: 'The S6 fidelity harness shows packed-call findings within tolerance of one-child-per-call.'", "parent c81c: 'S4 and S5 now DEPEND ON S6.' + 'Updated DAG: ... S6->{S4,S5}.'"]
  scenarios: ["S6 closes with its offline spec-discipline check green on every PR and the live semantic eval wired but never dispatched; S4 and S5 then close on their other ACs, marking 'The S6 fidelity harness shows findings within tolerance' as satisfied by inspection of an unrun harness — a fidelity regression in the merged/packed path ships unmeasured even though the DAG sequenced S6 first specifically to prevent that.", "A maintainer reads S4's 'S6 harness shows ... within tolerance' AC as binding, looks for the enforcement, and finds only S6's explicitly NON-BLOCKING / opt-in eval with no run-and-pass obligation — the gate the parent's eval-gating depends on is owned by no child's AC."]
  impact: "The epic's headline fidelity guarantee ('preserving (or improving) finding fidelity'; 'no fidelity regression') is the entire reason S4/S5 are eval-gated on S6. S4/S5 assume S6 supplies a BINDING gate, but S6's contract is an available-but-non-blocking tool with no must-run/must-pass AC. This dependency-strength mismatch means the eval-gate can be reported satisfied without the live fidelity diff ever running, defeating the sequencing the parent introduced."
  checklist_item: "Check the 7 cross-child modes: ... conflicting assumptions ... — each detected mode is a finding."
  suggested_fix: "Reconcile the gate-strength across the three children: add an explicit obligation that the S6 live semantic eval MUST be run and pass within tolerance as the human-reviewed gate for closing S4 and S5 — either (a) as a new S6 AC ('the live recall/attribution diff is run and shown within the documented tolerance before S4 and S5 are closed'), or (b) by rewording S4's and S5's 'S6 harness shows ... within tolerance' ACs to name the run-and-review obligation, while keeping S6's offline discipline check as spec-validity-only and the live eval non-blocking IN CI. This keeps 'non-blocking in CI' and 'binding human-reviewed dev gate' from contradicting each other across S6/S4/S5."

NOTE (relationship to the prior G3 audit on this child, 1782578715143742001): that G3 finding flagged the parent's AC4 WORDING ('within OFFLINE-eval tolerance') vs S6's mechanism (the fidelity measure is the LIVE eval). This G4 finding is distinct: it is a SIBLING-TO-SIBLING contract mismatch — S4/S5 assume S6's eval is a binding gate while S6 defines it as opt-in/non-blocking with no run-and-pass AC. The two are complementary (one is parent-criterion coverage, one is cross-child consistency) and share a root cause; (a) above fixes both.

compiled_state.description:
# Plan-review fidelity-eval: add G3/G4 to the eval suite (reuse existing infra; non-blocking)

## Why
S4 (merge G3+G4) and S5 (bin-packing) need a SEMANTIC check (real findings: new container path vs baseline).
rebar ALREADY has the eval infra — do NOT build a new harness:
- the Inspect-AI / promptfoo EVAL SEAM (workflow WS-G);
- the per-criterion plan-review eval suite `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/*.eval.yaml` (built under epic 5fd2);
- `src/rebar/llm/parity.py` parallel-run-and-diff (recall + false-accept on a gold set, `min_gold` floor).
The container criteria G3/G4 are NOT covered by any eval spec today — a real gap. This story FILLS it and plugs
into epic 6f2d (soupy-spool-revel) WS-EVAL-EXISTING's standing parallel-run-and-diff discipline (relates_to 6f2d).

## What
- Add G3/G4 (container) eval specs (e.g. `eval_specs/plan-review-container.eval.yaml`) with discrimination/recall
  fixtures over the shared corpuses (DSO read-only / snap-oakhart / rebar's own tickets), mirroring the existing specs.
- Reuse `parity.py` (recall / false-accept, `min_gold`) as the "merged-vs-separate" (S4) and "packed-vs-per-child"
  (S5) diff. Baseline = current separate-call (G3,G4)/one-child-per-call findings; candidate = merged/packed.
- Metric = finding recall + G3/G4 attribution accuracy; explicit, documented tolerance.

## CI posture (cautious: evals are non-deterministic + cost live $)
- The OFFLINE discipline check (spec validity + grader discipline; deterministic, no model) runs on every PR and
  is a REQUIRED check today — the new G3/G4 specs simply join it (cheap, safe to block).
- The LIVE semantic eval (billable, non-deterministic) stays OPT-IN — `workflow_dispatch`, optionally PATH-SCOPED
  to `reviewers/**` + `eval_specs/**` + `plan_review/**` so it never runs on irrelevant changes — and is NEVER a
  required/blocking CI status check.
- For S4/S5 the live eval is a HUMAN-REVIEWED DEVELOPMENT GATE (run it, review recall/attribution, pass before
  merge), NOT a CI blocker — a flaky run can't block the build and it costs nothing on unrelated PRs.

## Scope
- In: G3/G4 eval specs in the existing suite; reuse of parity.py + the Inspect seam; the candidate-vs-baseline
  metric/tolerance; the CI-posture wiring (offline discipline validates the new specs; live eval gated/path-scoped/
  non-blocking).
- Out: the merge (S4) / bin-pack (S5) implementations it gates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] G3/G4 container eval specs are added to `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs` and pass the OFFLINE discipline check
      (deterministic, runs on every PR).
- [ ] The eval reuses `parity.py` (recall + false-accept, `min_gold`) to diff a candidate container path vs the
      baseline, reporting finding-recall + G3/G4-attribution accuracy.
- [ ] An explicit numeric tolerance is defined + documented.
- [ ] The LIVE semantic eval is OPT-IN (workflow_dispatch / path-scoped) and NON-BLOCKING — NOT a required CI
      status check; documented as a human-reviewed development gate for S4/S5.
- [ ] No new live LLM cost is incurred on PRs that do not touch plan-review/reviewer/eval_specs.
- [ ] `make lint` + tests pass.

file_impact.reason: add G3/G4 container eval specs to the existing suite
reuse recall/false-accept diff for merged-vs-separate / packed-vs-per-child
offline discipline validates new specs; keep live eval opt-in/non-blocking/path-scoped

compiled_state.title: Plan-review fidelity-eval harness (semantic: new container path vs baseline)
